title: Classical Ciphers and Cryptanalysis as Defect Labs · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for classical ciphers and cryptanalysis as defect labs and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit native, entropy, interoperability, side-channel, compliance, or production transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for classical cipher attack and migration dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defe…
objectives: Implement and break a bounded shift or substitution cipher to connect keyspace, language redundancy, frequency evidence, and attacker cost.
Demonstrate how repeated XOR keystream use reveals relationships between plaintexts without presenting XOR alone as production encryption.
Explain one-time-pad key length, uniformity, secrecy, single use, distribution, and destruction requirements and their operational cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Crypto Go Caesar Frequency Break · changed case · Quiz Crypto Go Caesar Frequen…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 8329 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement and break a bounded shift or substitution cipher to connect keyspace, language redundancy, frequency evidence, and attacker cost.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 5855 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to model an authorized classical-cipher attack while quarantining all legacy use to migration or defect labs; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria.

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary, keep Crypto Go Legacy Migration Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria.
